FILE docs/protocol/realtime-websocket-protocol.md lines=1649
  1: ## Realtime WebSocket Protocol
  7: This document describes the current realtime WebSocket protocol between the Godot client and the Go game server.
  9: The protocol is JSON-over-WebSocket for auth, room, lobby, signaling, and other pre-readiness/session-control packets, and JSON-over-lane-specific WebRTC DataChannels for active realtime gameplay and tooling packets: `sr.world`, `sr.overlay`, `sr.session`, `sr.event`, `sr.asteroids.lifecycle`, and `sr.bullets.lifecycle` are ordered/reliable, `sr.asteroids` and `sr.bullets` are unordered/unreliable hot-update lanes, and the mandatory `sr.tooling` lane is reliable/ordered/bidirectional with negotiated id 9.
  11: It covers the transport route, JSON packet framing, connection lifecycle, packet-family routing, lane policy, gameplay packet families, session-state requirements, delivery semantics, source-of-truth files, generated outputs, service responsibilities, compatibility expectations, and implementation code paths.
  15: The realtime protocol currently uses JSON text messages over a WebSocket connection for signaling and queued one-off packets, and lane-specific WebRTC physical DataChannels for active realtime gameplay packets, with ordered/reliable lanes for `sr.world`, `sr.overlay`, `sr.session`, `sr.event`, `sr.asteroids.lifecycle`, and `sr.bullets.lifecycle`, unordered/unreliable hot-update lanes for `sr.asteroids` and `sr.bullets`, and a mandatory reliable/ordered/bidirectional `sr.tooling` transport lane at negotiated id 9.
  23: The Godot client selects a WebSocket URL from the requested session mode, opens the connection, optionally sends an auth packet, sends room or gameplay request packets, and receives authoritative server packets. /ws is the signaling, session, and control route, not the active gameplay state transport.
  25: WebSocket owns auth, room, lobby, signaling, and queued pre-readiness/session-control packets. WebRTC physical DataChannels own active realtime gameplay packets and runtime tooling packets. Current physical gameplay DataChannels are `sr.world`, `sr.overlay`, `sr.session`, `sr.event`, `sr.asteroids`, `sr.bullets`, `sr.asteroids.lifecycle`, and `sr.bullets.lifecycle`; `sr.tooling` is the ninth required channel and already carries runtime measurement. `control` is a logical recovery category, not a current physical WebRTC gameplay DataChannel. The current generated recovery packet families are `resync_request` and `resync_required`; there is no generated packet family named `control`, and there is no physical `sr.control` channel in the current implementation. The WebSocket URL may point at the normal hosted or proxied service route, but WebRTC DataChannel connectivity is established by ICE candidates rather than by a WebRTC URL. Deployment must allow the advertised WebRTC ICE address to reach the game server directly. Cloudflare-proxied HTTP routes should not be assumed to carry WebRTC DataChannel traffic.
  27: The route path does not define play mode. Local single-player and multiplayer currently use the same local WebSocket route during development. Single-player versus multiplayer behavior is expressed through packets, session identity, room state, admission policy, and player-data routing.
  29: The WebSocket connection itself is only transport readiness for WebSocket-owned packet families. It does not imply:
  33: room membership
  40: The server owns authority behind accepted room, gameplay, auth-result, telemetry, and devtools consequences. The client owns connection initiation, packet emission, inbound packet classification, realtime packet application through RealtimePacketPipeline, presentation routing, and WebRTC transport consumption.
  43: Active realtime gameplay packets are not WebSocket-owned anymore. The current server path builds the lane packet, encodes it to JSON, and sends it over the matching session WebRTC gameplay DataChannel when WebRTC is ready. WebSocket still owns auth, room/lobby lifecycle, room snapshots, and WebRTC signaling. There is no WebSocket fallback for active realtime gameplay packets.
  44: The protocol is best-effort and session-scoped, but active gameplay output now uses lane-native packet families and lane policy over WebRTC DataChannels. The current gameplay output lanes are:
  51: session
  57: `sr.tooling` is not an active gameplay packet lane. Its negotiated channel is mandatory for readiness, and tooling packets are separated before normal gameplay routing. Runtime measurement request/response traffic and runtime devtools command requests are implemented on this channel. Devtools command payloads carry `request_id` and `trace_id`; `services/game-server/internal/networking/tooling` applies packet-policy, room, and capability preflight, decodes `DebugCommand`, dispatches through the existing devtools controller, and returns correlated `tooling_command_result` or `tooling_error` packets. Telemetry routing exists but remains partially wired. Developer readouts now use `sr.tooling`; the legacy continuous telemetry/ping path remains to migrate.
  72: session_full
  73: session_delta
  77: These packets carry current lane snapshots, baseline updates, and event batches instead of one combined lane gameplay output payload. `resync_request` and `resync_required` are WebSocket control packets, not active WebRTC lane outputs or `RealtimeLaneCandidate`s.
  79: Every server-built realtime packet carries the authoritative `match_id` metadata (compact wire key `mid`). A `RealtimeSessionState` is bounded by the identity tuple `(ReceiverID, MatchID)`; changing either component starts clean lane, baseline, projection, and hot-lane cohort state.
  81: WebSocket room snapshots and WebRTC gameplay packets have no cross-transport ordering guarantee. The client pipeline expands compact `mid` to readable `match_id` and validates recognized packet types before applying its match boundary. Before an authoritative active match exists, recognized packets with a non-empty `match_id` are buffered by match and do not mutate lane or presentation state; missing IDs are rejected. When the authoritative `InGame` room snapshot activates `begin_realtime_match(match_id)`, the pipeline clears all pending match buckets and replays only that match's packets through normal lane routing. Pending unrelated matches are discarded, and once a match is active, mismatched IDs are rejected. `GameOver` retains the active match until Lobby/session teardown; `end_match`, reset, and connection teardown clear pending packets and protocol state. This applies to all active packet families, including lifecycle and event packets.

1:## Spectate Session And Camera Flow
5:## Purpose
7:This document describes the current client spectate session and camera handoff flow.
9:## Overview
11:The client spectate flow owns the availability of spectate targets, the cycling of those targets, the handoff into spectate mode, and the camera/view-target handoff that follows from the selected spectate target.
13:`GameplaySpectateContext` wires the spectate flow to gameplay menu state and world sync. `SpectateSessionFlow` keeps the spectate menu state updated from gameplay state. `GameplaySpectateFlow` starts spectating, advances to the next target, opens the spectate menu when spectating is active, and hands off the selected target to world sync and camera focus.
17:## Code root
20:client/
26:client/scripts/gameplay/spectate/
27:client/scripts/gameplay/
28:client/scripts/ui/menu_flow/
31:## Responsibilities
33:The client spectate session and camera flow owns:
35:* collecting spectate-available player ids from gameplay state
36:* excluding the local player from spectate target availability
38:* beginning spectate mode from the current available target
39:* cycling to the next available spectate target
40:* syncing spectate menu state from gameplay state
41:* opening spectate menu state when spectating is active
44:* keeping spectate selection separate from gameplay targeting rules
46:## Does not own
48:The client spectate session and camera flow does not own:
58:## Flow behavior
60:### Spectate target availability
62:`SpectateMenuState` reads `self_id` and `player_lifecycle` from gameplay state and builds the available spectate target list from that lifecycle data.
70:If no available targets remain, spectate cannot begin.
72:### Beginning spectate mode
76:If world sync accepts the target focus, the flow marks spectate mode active.
78:### Cycling targets
80:`GameplaySpectateFlow.request_cycle_target()` advances to the next available spectate target only while spectating is already active.
84:### Menu-state integration
86:`SpectateSessionFlow.apply_gameplay_state()` keeps the spectate menu state aligned with incoming gameplay state.
88:`GameplaySpectateContext.configure()` wires the spectate request signal from the menu flow so the spectate flow can begin spectating from the current menu state.
90:### World-sync and camera handoff
92:The spectate flow hands the chosen target to world sync through the view-target path and then hands the same target to the camera focus path.
94:That handoff keeps spectate presentation aligned with the selected player instead of treating spectate selection as a gameplay target request.
96:## Code map
101:client/scripts/gameplay/spectate/gameplay_spectate_context.gd
102:client/scripts/gameplay/spectate/gameplay_spectate_flow.gd
103:client/scripts/gameplay/spectate/spectate_menu_state.gd
104:client/scripts/gameplay/spectate/spectate_session_flow.gd
107:## Tests
109:No focused test is documented yet for this flow.
111:The closest verification boundary is the spectate flow implementation in:
114:client/scripts/gameplay/spectate/
117:## Related docs
127:## Notes
129:This document captures current spectate session and camera handoff behavior. It stays separate from gameplay targeting and does not describe future menu or spectate policy.

1:# Runtime and Report Flow
5:## Purpose
7:The diagnostic aggregator is the bounded service for triggered diagnostic reports. It accepts diagnostic material, applies validation and safety policy, constructs a finalized report, stores it for bounded retrieval, and exposes the report API.
9:## Overview
11:Diagnostic-aggregator is logically independent and currently co-hosted by the game-server process. The game-server composition root hosts the routes and process lifecycle; diagnostic-aggregator owns report processing and storage. There is no standalone diagnostic-aggregator process. Producer integration beyond the manual producer remains deferred.
13:## Co-hosted Runtime
15:There is no standalone diagnostic-aggregator executable, listener, process configuration package, process logger, service identity package, or standalone health/readiness surface. The manual producer remains at `services/diagnostic-aggregator/cmd/diagnostic-submit/`.
23:  -> constructs hosted diagnostic-aggregator when enabled
24:  -> registers /v1/diagnostic-reports and /v1/diagnostic-reports/
36:The default storage root is `data/diagnostic-reports`, the default request limit is 4 MiB, and the default report retention is 14 days. The shared game-server base URL is currently `http://127.0.0.1:8080`; report routes are under `/v1/diagnostic-reports`.
38:## Permitted Co-hosting Boundary
40:Co-hosting permits only composition-root construction, registration, and closure. The sole external Go import is the game-server composition adapter importing the public `services/diagnostic-aggregator/hosted` package. Game-server internal/runtime/domain packages and every player-data package must not import any diagnostic-aggregator package.
42:Game-server and player-data must submit or retrieve reports only through a transport/client implementation of the diagnostic-report HTTP API. They must never call diagnostic handlers, application services, or report stores directly. Diagnostic handlers, services, stores, and internal types must not be passed into game-server or player-data constructors.
51:The diagnostic-aggregator owns:
63:The diagnostic package does not listen, create an HTTP server, handle signals, forward continuous logs, or shut down the game-server process.
65:## Report Flow
71:  -> bounded diagnostic submission to the shared game-server base URL
76:  -> finalized diagnostic report construction
78:  -> retrieval by the diagnostic-report surface
81:Reports are not a continuous stream. Ordinary service logs remain owned by their producing services and are not continuously forwarded to diagnostic-aggregator. Diagnostic failure must not affect gameplay.
83:## Code root
85:`services/diagnostic-aggregator/`
87:## Responsibilities
89:Diagnostic-aggregator owns diagnostic-report route registration, bearer authentication, submission validation, safety inspection, report construction, bounded storage and retrieval, retention enforcement, and report-store closure. The game-server composition root owns shared HTTP process composition, route hosting, process signals, and server shutdown.
91:## Does not own
95:## Domain roles
97:Diagnostic-aggregator is the bounded diagnostic-report processing and storage authority. The game-server composition root is a host and lifecycle coordinator only; it does not gain report-processing authority. Producers and clients provide bounded diagnostic submissions through the HTTP API.
99:Co-hosting permits only composition-root construction, registration, and closure. The sole external Go import is the game-server composition adapter importing the public `services/diagnostic-aggregator/hosted` package. Game-server internal/runtime/domain packages and every player-data package must not import any diagnostic-aggregator package. Direct handler, application-service, report-store, or internal-type calls and constructor injection remain forbidden.
101:## Protocols and APIs
103:The diagnostic-report HTTP API exists to submit bounded diagnostic material and retrieve accepted reports. Manual producers and future transport/client implementations are callers. Diagnostic-aggregator handlers and application services are authoritative for validation, safety policy, report construction, and processing; the report store is authoritative for bounded persistence and retrieval.
105:Data crossing the boundary includes the authenticated submission envelope, bounded diagnostic payload, correlation fields, and report retrieval identifiers/results. The API does not own gameplay state, player-data persistence, match outcomes, continuous logs, or caller-side retry and degradation policy.
107:Game-server and player-data must submit or retrieve reports only through a bounded transport/client implementation of this HTTP API, never through direct imports or calls to diagnostic handlers, application services, stores, or internal types. Those diagnostic objects must not be passed into their constructors.
109:## Canonical Operational Events
111:The hosted service owns diagnostic-aggregator lifecycle emission. Startup creates one trace for `service_starting`, initialization failures, and `service_started`. Shutdown creates a different trace for `service_stopping`, report-store close failure, and `service_stopped`. The game-server host does not duplicate the aggregator's successful-start event; it emits only the disabled configuration decision and any additional host-level close dependency failure.
113:Each aggregator HTTP request receives a new aggregator-owned request ID and provisional trace ID. Once a request body has been strictly decoded, a valid submitted `correlation.trace_id` replaces the provisional trace for the remaining intake operation. Submitted request, session, room, match, player, and account correlation stays inside the diagnostic report and does not become the aggregator HTTP request identity.
117:- the HTTP handler emits `diagnostic_report_rejected` for transport, decoding, envelope, safety, and useful identifier rejection paths;
118:- the report service emits one `aggregator_event_accepted` after successful validation, `diagnostic_report_stored` after durable save, and `aggregator_storage_failed` for save, non-not-found load, or corrupt stored-report failures;
124:Current production diagnostic-aggregator code has zero `log_message` bridge sites. Repository-wide bridge retirement remains deferred until the other services complete their workflow migrations.
126:## Data ownership
128:Diagnostic-aggregator owns validated report contents, safety decisions, finalized report shape, report identifiers, bounded JSONL storage, and retention metadata. Producers own the source diagnostic material and correlation context they submit. The game-server owns process and gameplay data; player-data owns player-data aggregates and persistence. Neither service gains diagnostic-storage ownership by co-hosting or producing a request.
130:## Storage and Retention
132:The diagnostic-aggregator owns bounded report storage for accepted reports. The default retention window is 14 days and is enforced when the hosted service starts. This storage is diagnostic evidence, not authoritative audit storage.
134:## Future Detachment
138:## Code map
140:- `services/diagnostic-aggregator/` - diagnostic-report service implementation.
141:- `services/diagnostic-aggregator/hosted` - public hosted-service adapter.
142:- `services/diagnostic-aggregator/cmd/diagnostic-submit/` - current manual producer.
143:- `services/game-server/cmd/game-server/` - composition-root route hosting and lifecycle wiring.
145:## Tests
147:Tests should cover bounded HTTP submission and retrieval, validation and safety rejection, report processing, storage/retention behavior, and hosted construction/registration/closure. Boundary tests must preserve transport-only producer access and must not introduce direct diagnostic dependencies into game-server or player-data runtime/domain code.
149:## Related docs
152:- [Game-server diagnostic-aggregator hosting](../game-server/integrations/diagnostic-aggregator-hosting.md)
154:- [Observability planning](../../planning/domains/technical/observability-logging-and-diagnostics.md)

1:# Diagnostic-Aggregator Hosting
5:## Purpose
7:This document defines the game-server process boundary for temporarily co-hosting the logically independent diagnostic-aggregator service. It records the permitted composition-root hosting exception and the forbidden runtime and domain dependencies.
9:## Overview
11:`diagnostic-aggregator` is a separate service with its own diagnostic application and report-storage responsibilities. It is temporarily co-hosted by the game-server process for deployment and process-composition reasons. Co-hosting does not make diagnostic-aggregator part of the game-server runtime, gameplay domain, networking domain, rooms, match reporting, or player-data domain.
16:services/game-server/cmd/game-server/ -> services/diagnostic-aggregator/hosted
19:Only the game-server composition-root hosting adapter under `services/game-server/cmd/game-server/` may import `services/diagnostic-aggregator/hosted`.
21:No package under `services/game-server/internal/`, no gameplay, networking, rooms, or match-reporting package, and no player-data package may import any diagnostic-aggregator package.
23:No game-server or player-data runtime/domain code may call diagnostic handlers, report services, stores, or other diagnostic-aggregator internals in-process.
30:-> diagnostic-aggregator handler/application service
38:-> diagnostic application service/store/direct handler call
41:The composition root may construct, register, and close the hosted service. That lifecycle authority does not grant report-processing authority or permission to make direct diagnostic calls. The composition root only assembles the process and its transport wiring.
43:Future detachment changes process composition and service addressing only. Callers continue through the transport/API contract; detachment must not require changing game-server or player-data runtime/domain callers to use diagnostic-aggregator internals.
45:## Responsibilities
49:- Constructing the hosted diagnostic-aggregator service through its hosting adapter.
54:The diagnostic-aggregator service is responsible for:
56:- Receiving diagnostic requests through its HTTP transport/API.
57:- Running diagnostic handler and application-service behavior.
58:- Validating and aggregating diagnostic reports.
61:Producers and clients are responsible for sending diagnostic information through the HTTP transport/API contract.
63:## Code root
65:`services/game-server/`
67:## Does not own
76:- A direct in-process diagnostic call path.
79:## Domain roles
81:The game-server composition root is a process host and lifecycle coordinator. It may construct, register, and close diagnostic-aggregator, but it does not become the diagnostic report-processing authority. Diagnostic-aggregator remains authoritative for diagnostic handlers, application services, validation, report construction, and report storage. Game-server gameplay, networking, rooms, match reporting, and player-data remain separate domain owners.
83:## Data ownership
85:Diagnostic-aggregator owns diagnostic report processing, finalized report data, report identifiers, retention, and report-store data. Producers own the diagnostic material and correlation context they submit. The game server owns gameplay and process data; player-data owns player-data aggregates and persistence. Co-hosting does not transfer these ownership responsibilities.
87:## Protocols and APIs
89:The integration boundary is the diagnostic-aggregator HTTP transport/API. Producers and clients send requests to that API, and the diagnostic-aggregator handler/application service processes them before writing to the report store.
91:Game-server and player-data code must remain callers through that transport/API contract when they participate in a permitted producer flow. They must not import or call diagnostic handlers, application services, stores, or internals directly in-process.
95:## Code map
99:- `services/game-server/cmd/game-server/` - composition-root hosting adapter; the only game-server location permitted to import `services/diagnostic-aggregator/hosted`.
100:- `services/diagnostic-aggregator/hosted` - hosted-service adapter used for temporary process co-hosting.
104:- `services/game-server/internal/` - no diagnostic-aggregator imports.
105:- Game-server gameplay, networking, rooms, and match-reporting packages - no diagnostic-aggregator imports or direct calls.
106:- Player-data packages - no diagnostic-aggregator imports or direct calls.
108:The exact handler, application-service, and report-store implementation paths belong to the diagnostic-aggregator service and are not game-server integration dependencies.
110:## Tests
112:Tests for this boundary should verify the HTTP transport/API behavior and service lifecycle wiring without granting runtime or domain code a direct diagnostic dependency. Co-hosting tests may verify construction, registration, request routing, and close behavior at the composition root.
116:## Related docs
123:## Notes
125:Co-hosting is a deployment arrangement, not an ownership merger. The game-server composition root may host the service, but diagnostic-aggregator remains logically independent and retains authority over diagnostic report processing and storage.

  1: ## Pregame Mode And Actions
  2: 
  3: Parent index: [Pregame Menu Flow](./!INDEX.md)
  4: 
  5: ## Purpose
  6: 
  7: This document describes the client implementation responsibility for pregame mode presentation and menu action routing.
  8: 
  9: ## Overview
 10: 
 11: `PregameMenu` owns the current single-player and multiplayer pregame mode presentation.
 12: 
 13: `PregameModePresenter` applies mode-specific labels, visible button text, and disabled future actions for the current mode. `PregameMenuMode` provides the mode values used by the presenter and menu action routing.
 14: 
 15: Single-player and multiplayer share the same pregame surface, but the current mode changes the visible labels and which menu actions are routed when the user presses the available buttons.
 16: 
 17: The pregame menu also hands off transmission-panel-related actions to the transmission flow and the focused pregame subflows when those actions are requested. This document keeps those handoffs at a high level and does not restate transmission-panel mounting details.
 18: 
 19: ## Code root
 20: 
 21: ```text
 22: client/
 23: ```
 24: 
 25: Primary implementation areas:
 26: 
 27: ```text
 28: client/scripts/ui/menus/
 29: client/scripts/ui/menu_flow/
 30: client/scenes/ui/
 31: ```
 32: 
 33: ## Responsibilities
 34: 
 35: The client pregame mode and action routing flow owns:
 36: 
 37: * presenting the current pregame mode as single-player or multiplayer
 38: * applying mode-specific button label swaps
 39: * disabling future buttons that are not currently available
 40: * routing single-player Play Endless actions
 41: * routing multiplayer Create, Join, and Logout actions
 42: * routing profile and local-pilot requests to the correct pregame subflows
 43: * keeping mode presentation aligned with the active pregame mode
 44: 
 45: ## Does not own
 46: 
 47: The client pregame mode and action routing flow does not own:
 48: 
 49: * transmission panel mounting details
 50: * local pilot selection policy
 51: * profile readout shaping
 52: * lobby transport
 53: * auth session authority
 54: * gameplay runtime
 55: * server state
 56: 
 57: ## Mode behavior
 58: 
 59: ### Single-player mode
 60: 
 61: Single-player mode shows the single-player labels, keeps the Play Endless action available, and routes the select-pilot action to the local pilot flow.
 62: 
 63: The single-player view keeps the multiplayer-specific actions hidden or disabled and leaves future buttons disabled.
 64: 
 65: ### Multiplayer mode
 66: 
 67: Multiplayer mode shows the multiplayer labels, keeps the Create and Join actions available, and routes Logout through the multiplayer pregame path.
 68: 
 69: The multiplayer view hides the single-player-specific labels and leaves future buttons disabled.
 70: 
 71: ## Action routing
 72: 
 73: `PregameMenu` routes button presses through the current mode:
 74: 
 75: * Play Endless routes from single-player mode.
 76: * Create routes from multiplayer mode.
 77: * Join routes from multiplayer mode.
 78: * Logout routes from multiplayer mode.
 79: * Select Pilot routes from single-player mode.
 80: * Profile routes independently of mode as a pregame profile request.
 81: 
 82: The menu keeps routing state aligned with `PregameMenuMode` so a button only emits the action that is valid for the active mode.
 83: 
 84: ## Code map
 85: 
 86: Primary implementation files:
 87: 
 88: ```text
 89: client/scripts/ui/menus/pregame_menu.gd
 90: client/scripts/ui/menus/pregame_mode_presenter.gd
 91: client/scripts/ui/menu_flow/pregame_menu_mode.gd
 92: ```
 93: 
 94: ## Tests
 95: 
 96: No focused test is documented yet for this flow.
 97: 
 98: The closest verification boundary is the pregame menu implementation in:
 99: 
100: ```text
101: client/scripts/ui/menus/pregame_menu.gd
102: ```
103: 
104: ## Related docs

=== docs/services/client/spectate-flow/spectate-session-and-camera-flow.md ===
  1: ## Spectate Session And Camera Flow
  2: 
  3: Parent index: [Spectate Flow](./!INDEX.md)
  4: 
  5: ## Purpose
  6: 
  7: This document describes the current client spectate session and camera handoff flow.
  8: 
  9: ## Overview
 10: 
 11: The client spectate flow owns the availability of spectate targets, the cycling of those targets, the handoff into spectate mode, and the camera/view-target handoff that follows from the selected spectate target.
 12: 
 13: `GameplaySpectateContext` wires the spectate flow to gameplay menu state and world sync. `SpectateSessionFlow` keeps the spectate menu state updated from gameplay state. `GameplaySpectateFlow` starts spectating, advances to the next target, opens the spectate menu when spectating is active, and hands off the selected target to world sync and camera focus.
 14: 
 15: Spectate target selection is separate from gameplay targeting. Spectate uses player lifecycle availability and view-target handoff, while gameplay targeting continues to own combat or interaction target rules.
 16: 
 17: ## Code root
 18: 
 19: ```text
 20: client/
 21: ```
 22: 
 23: Primary implementation areas:
 24: 
 25: ```text
 26: client/scripts/gameplay/spectate/
 27: client/scripts/gameplay/
 28: client/scripts/ui/menu_flow/
 29: ```
 30: 
 31: ## Responsibilities
 32: 
 33: The client spectate session and camera flow owns:
 34: 
 35: * collecting spectate-available player ids from gameplay state
 36: * excluding the local player from spectate target availability
 37: * excluding dead and game-over players where lifecycle state marks them unavailable
 38: * beginning spectate mode from the current available target
 39: * cycling to the next available spectate target
 40: * syncing spectate menu state from gameplay state
 41: * opening spectate menu state when spectating is active
 42: * handing the chosen target to world sync as the active view target
 43: * handing the chosen target to the camera focus path
 44: * keeping spectate selection separate from gameplay targeting rules
 45: 
 46: ## Does not own
 47: 
 48: The client spectate session and camera flow does not own:
 49: 
 50: * server player authority
 51: * gameplay targeting rules
 52: * world-sync entity synchronization generally
 53: * gameplay menu presentation generally
 54: * combat target selection
 55: * server-side lifecycle policy
 56: * devtools behavior
 57: 
 58: ## Flow behavior
 59: 
 60: ### Spectate target availability
 61: 
 62: `SpectateMenuState` reads `self_id` and `player_lifecycle` from gameplay state and builds the available spectate target list from that lifecycle data.
 63: 
 64: The current implementation excludes:
 65: 
 66: * the local player
 67: * players marked `Dead`
 68: * players marked `GameOver`
 69: 
 70: If no available targets remain, spectate cannot begin.
 71: 
 72: ### Beginning spectate mode
 73: 
 74: `GameplaySpectateFlow.begin_spectating()` asks the menu state for the current target and then hands that target to world sync.
 75: 
 76: If world sync accepts the target focus, the flow marks spectate mode active.
 77: 
 78: ### Cycling targets
 79: 
 80: `GameplaySpectateFlow.request_cycle_target()` advances to the next available spectate target only while spectating is already active.
 81: 
 82: The flow updates world sync with the new view target and then requests camera focus for the same player id.
 83: 
 84: ### Menu-state integration
 85: 
 86: `SpectateSessionFlow.apply_gameplay_state()` keeps the spectate menu state aligned with incoming gameplay state.
 87: 
 88: `GameplaySpectateContext.configure()` wires the spectate request signal from the menu flow so the spectate flow can begin spectating from the current menu state.
 89: 
 90: ### World-sync and camera handoff
 91: 
 92: The spectate flow hands the chosen target to world sync through the view-target path and then hands the same target to the camera focus path.
 93: 
 94: That handoff keeps spectate presentation aligned with the selected player instead of treating spectate selection as a gameplay target request.
 95: 
 96: ## Code map
 97: 
 98: Primary implementation files:
 99: 
100: ```text
101: client/scripts/gameplay/spectate/gameplay_spectate_context.gd
102: client/scripts/gameplay/spectate/gameplay_spectate_flow.gd
103: client/scripts/gameplay/spectate/spectate_menu_state.gd
104: client/scripts/gameplay/spectate/spectate_session_flow.gd
105: ```
106: 
107: ## Tests
108: 
109: No focused test is documented yet for this flow.
110: 
111: The closest verification boundary is the spectate flow implementation in:
112: 
113: ```text
114: client/scripts/gameplay/spectate/
115: ```
116:

126: 
127: ## Notes
128: 
129: This document captures current spectate session and camera handoff behavior. It stays separate from gameplay targeting and does not describe future menu or spectate policy.

 126: 				return

 128: 			return
 129: 	if gameplay_composition == null:
 130: 		return
 131: 	if gameplay_composition.handle_gameplay_input(event):
 132: 		get_viewport().set_input_as_handled()

 274: func _create_match_controller() -> Array:
 275: 	var connection := FakeConnectionService.new()
 276: 	add_child_autofree(connection)
 277: 	var room := FakeRoomSessionController.new()
 278: 	var gameplay := FakeGameplaySessionController.new()
 279: 	gameplay.set_events(connection.events)
 280: 	var controller := SessionNetworkController.new()
 281: 	controller.configure(connection, null, {})
 282: 	controller.configure_room_session_controller(room)
 283: 	controller.configure_gameplay_session_controller(gameplay)
 284: 	controller.connect_connection_signals()
 285: 	controller.connect_room_signals()
 286: 	return [controller, connection, room, gameplay]

 293: func test_match_boundaries_order_idempotency_and_lobby_cleanup() -> void:
 294: 	var setup := _create_match_controller()
 295: 	var controller: SessionNetworkController = setup[0]
 296: 	var connection: FakeConnectionService = setup[1]
 297: 
 298: 	controller._on_room_snapshot_received(_match_snapshot(Constants.ROOM_STATE_IN_GAME, "match-1"))
 299: 	assert_eq(connection.events, ["gameplay_reset", "begin:match-1", "gameplay_accept"])
 300: 	controller._on_room_snapshot_received(_match_snapshot(Constants.ROOM_STATE_IN_GAME, "match-1"))
 301: 	assert_eq(connection.events, ["gameplay_reset", "begin:match-1", "gameplay_accept", "gameplay_accept"])
 302: 	controller._on_room_snapshot_received(_match_snapshot(Constants.ROOM_STATE_GAME_OVER, "match-1"))
 303: 	controller._on_room_state_changed(_match_snapshot(Constants.ROOM_STATE_GAME_OVER, "match-1"))
 304: 	assert_eq(connection.events.size(), 4)
 305: 	controller._on_room_snapshot_received(_match_snapshot(Constants.ROOM_STATE_LOBBY, "match-1"))
 306: 	assert_eq(connection.events, ["gameplay_reset", "begin:match-1", "gameplay_accept", "gameplay_accept", "gameplay_reset", "end"])
 307: 	controller._on_room_snapshot_received(_match_snapshot(Constants.ROOM_STATE_LOBBY, "match-1"))
 308: 	controller._on_room_state_changed(_match_snapshot(Constants.ROOM_STATE_LOBBY, "match-1"))
 309: 	assert_eq(connection.events.size(), 6)
 310: 	controller._on_room_snapshot_received(_match_snapshot(Constants.ROOM_STATE_IN_GAME, "match-2"))
 311: 	assert_eq(connection.events.slice(6), ["gameplay_reset", "begin:match-2", "gameplay_accept"])

   1: extends RefCounted
   2: class_name SpectateMenuState
   3: 
   4: # Owns spectate availability/menu state. This is the future home for outside
   5: # spectating rules; it does not render UI or send packets.
   6: 
   7: var self_id := ""
   8: var current_target_id := ""
   9: var player_lifecycle := {}
  10: 
  11: 
  12: func apply_gameplay_state(state: Dictionary) -> void:
  13: 	self_id = str(state.get("self_id", ""))
  14: 	var lifecycle = state.get("player_lifecycle", {})
  15: 	if lifecycle is Dictionary:
  16: 		player_lifecycle = lifecycle
  17: 	else:
  18: 		player_lifecycle = {}
  19: 
  20: 
  21: func reset() -> void:
  22: 	self_id = ""
  23: 	current_target_id = ""
  24: 	player_lifecycle.clear()
  25: 
  26: 
  27: func spectate_target_ids() -> Array:
  28: 	var target_ids := []
  29: 	for player_id in player_lifecycle.keys():
  30: 		if player_id == self_id:
  31: 			continue
  32: 
  33: 		var lifecycle_value := str(player_lifecycle[player_id])
  34: 		if lifecycle_value == "Dead" || lifecycle_value == "GameOver":
  35: 			continue
  36: 
  37: 		target_ids.append(player_id)
  38: 	return target_ids
  39: 
  40: 
  41: func current_target() -> String:
  42: 	var target_ids := spectate_target_ids()
  43: 	if current_target_id in target_ids:
  44: 		return current_target_id
  45: 	if target_ids.is_empty():
  46: 		return ""
  47: 	return str(target_ids[0])
  48: 
  49: 
  50: func begin_spectating() -> String:
  51: 	current_target_id = current_target()
  52: 	return current_target_id
  53: 
  54: 
  55: func cycle_next_target() -> String:
  56: 	var target_ids := spectate_target_ids()
  57: 	if target_ids.is_empty():
  58: 		current_target_id = ""
  59: 		return current_target_id
  60: 
  61: 	var current_index := target_ids.find(current_target_id)
  62: 	if current_index == -1:
  63: 		current_target_id = str(target_ids[0])
  64: 		return current_target_id
  65: 
  66: 	current_target_id = str(target_ids[(current_index + 1) % target_ids.size()])
  67: 	return current_target_id

  70: func has_spectate_targets() -> bool:

 122: 			for asteroid_id in world_lane_state.dirty_asteroid_ids.keys():
 123: 				if world_lane_state.asteroids.has(asteroid_id):
 124: 					asteroid_sync.apply_asteroid(str(asteroid_id), world_lane_state.asteroids[asteroid_id], player_render_api.visual_position(), player_render_api.server_position())
 125: 			world_lane_state.clear_asteroid_change_sets()
 126: 	if pickup_sync != null:
 127: 		pickup_sync.remove_missing(world_lane_state.pickups)
 128: 		pickup_sync.apply(world_lane_state.pickups, player_render_api.visual_position(), player_render_api.server_position())

 131: func reset() -> void:
 132: 	set_current_self_id("")
 133: 	if player_render_api != null:
 134: 		player_render_api.reset()
 135: 	if projectile_sync != null:
 136: 		projectile_sync.reset()
 137: 	if asteroid_sync != null:

 145: func apply_state(
 146: 	self_id: String,
 147: 	server_players: Dictionary,
 148: 	server_bullets: Dictionary,
 149: 	server_asteroids: Dictionary,
 150: 	server_pickups: Dictionary = {}
 151: ) -> void:
 152: 	current_self_id = self_id
 153: 
 154: 	if target_position_source != null:
 155: 		target_position_source.set_current_self_id(self_id)
 156: 	player_render_api.remove_missing(server_players, self_id)
 157: 	projectile_sync.remove_missing(server_bullets)
 158: 	asteroid_sync.remove_missing(server_asteroids)
 159: 	pickup_sync.remove_missing(server_pickups)
 160: 	player_render_api.apply_state(self_id, server_players)
 161: 	projectile_sync.apply(
 162: 		server_bullets,
 163: 		player_render_api.visual_position(),
 164: 		player_render_api.server_position()
 165: 	)
 166: 	asteroid_sync.apply(
 167: 		server_asteroids,
 168: 		player_render_api.visual_position(),
 169: 		player_render_api.server_position()
 170: 	)
 171: 	pickup_sync.apply(
 172: 		server_pickups,
 173: 		player_render_api.visual_position(),
 174: 		player_render_api.server_position()
 175: 	)

 178: func interpolate(delta: float) -> void:
 179: 	var weight := 1.0 - exp(-Constants.PLAYER_INTERPOLATION_SPEED * delta)
 180: 	player_render_api.interpolate(weight, current_self_id)
 181: 	projectile_sync.interpolate(weight)
 182: 	asteroid_sync.interpolate(weight)
 183: 	pickup_sync.interpolate(weight)

48:## Responsibilities
58:## Code root
63:## Domain roles
69:`ClientConnectionService` creates the `NetworkClient`, installs signal connections, and exposes a stable service surface to session, room, gameplay, telemetry, and devtools code.
71:`ClientConnectionService` configures callbacks on `ClientInboundCoordinator` and `RealtimeTransportSession`. `ClientConnectionService` owns application-facing non-realtime dispatcher bindings and public facade signals. `ClientInboundCoordinator` owns only the five WebRTC control dispatcher bindings and the handlers exposed by `RealtimeTransportSession`. `RealtimePacketPipeline` owns gameplay lane dispatcher bindings. `NetworkClient` does not bind directly to the realtime transport, pipeline, or session collaborators; it only emits transport and decoded-packet signals to the facade.
99:    owns gameplay lane dispatcher bindings, realtime gameplay packet application, and readiness
105:## Protocols and APIs
107:### Connection startup
130:### Poll loop
160:### Open-state behavior
183:active gameplay state
189:### Close behavior
222:### Realtime reset lifecycle
237:`RealtimePacketPipeline.reset()` replaces the active `RealtimeRouter`. That replacement clears world/overlay/session protocol state, pending lifecycle baseline buckets, pending lifecycle duplicate tracking, the latest applied asteroid lifecycle sequence, and the latest applied bullet lifecycle sequence. This reset is connection-scoped and is performed on connect and close; `GameplaySessionController.reset()` is a gameplay-session reset and does not perform this protocol reset.
243:### Graceful close
274:### Packet decode lifecycle
332:    -> gameplay packet application
335:Application-facing non-realtime packet-specific public facade signals arrive from `ClientConnectionService` bindings. WebRTC control outputs are consumed by `ClientInboundCoordinator`, and realtime gameplay lane outputs are consumed by `RealtimePacketPipeline`. Connection-level `NetworkClient` signals remain separate from these packet routes. Detailed classification belongs to inbound packet routing rather than this lifecycle document.

546:## Notes
550:A successful WebSocket connection is only transport readiness. Authentication, room membership, gameplay participation, and player authority are separate states.
560:This document describes where networking logs are emitted from current networking paths. Client logging implementation details belong to [Client Logging](../client-logging.md), packet schema details belong to protocol docs, and logs do not own packet routing or gameplay consequences.

 456: 
 457: ## Data ownership
 458: 
 459: `NetworkClient` owns transient transport state such as the live `WebSocketPeer`, connection flags, graceful-close state, and wire send/receive handoff.
 460: 
 461: `ClientConnectionService` owns the cached websocket auth identity fields that are cleared on close and the lifecycle signals exposed to the rest of the client.
 462: 
 463: Packet dictionaries and encoded wire text are transport payloads only. This document does not own packet schema, room state, gameplay state, or account identity.

"""Validate and merge codemap precision curation shards."""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import hashlib
import json
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

LABELS = {"valid_missing_link", "plausible_but_unnecessary", "incorrect"}
AUDIT_FIELDS = {
    "document_section",
    "document_ref",
    "document_excerpt",
    "target_ref",
    "target_excerpt",
    "target_sha256",
    "target_kind",
}
MUTABLE_FIELDS = {"label", "rationale", "audit"}


def read(path: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:
    with path.open(encoding="utf-8") as stream:
        value = json.load(stream)
    if not isinstance(value, dict) or not isinstance(value.get("suggestions"), list):
        raise ValueError(f"invalid benchmark JSON: {path}")
    return value


def pair(item: dict[str, Any]) -> tuple[str, str]:
    return str(item.get("document", "")), str(item.get("target", ""))


def immutable(item: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
    return {key: value for key, value in item.items() if key not in MUTABLE_FIELDS}


def file_hash(path: Path) -> str:
    digest = hashlib.sha256()
    with path.open("rb") as stream:
        for block in iter(lambda: stream.read(1024 * 1024), b""):
            digest.update(block)
    return digest.hexdigest()


def directory_hash(root: Path, directory: Path) -> str:
    rows: list[str] = []
    for child in sorted(path for path in directory.rglob("*") if path.is_file()):
        rows.append(f"{child.relative_to(root).as_posix()}:{file_hash(child)}")
    return hashlib.sha256("\n".join(rows).encode()).hexdigest()


def verify_target(root: Path, item: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
    audit = item["audit"]
    target = root.joinpath(*str(item["target"]).split("/"))
    kind = audit["target_kind"]
    expected = audit["target_sha256"]
    if target.is_file():
        if kind != "file":
            raise ValueError(f"{pair(item)} target kind {kind!r}, expected file")
        actual = file_hash(target)
    elif target.is_dir():
        if kind != "directory":
            raise ValueError(f"{pair(item)} target kind {kind!r}, expected directory")
        actual = directory_hash(root, target)
    else:
        if kind != "missing":
            raise ValueError(f"{pair(item)} target does not exist but kind is {kind!r}")
        actual = ""
    if expected != actual:
        raise ValueError(f"{pair(item)} target SHA mismatch: {expected!r} != {actual!r}")


def merge(source: Path, shards: list[Path], repository: Path, output: Path, reviewed_at: str) -> dict[str, int]:
    template = read(source)
    envelope = {key: template[key] for key in ("schema_version", "corpus", "sampling")}
    expected = {pair(item): item for item in template["suggestions"]}
    if len(expected) != len(template["suggestions"]):
        raise ValueError("template contains duplicate document-target pairs")

    merged: dict[tuple[str, str], dict[str, Any]] = {}
    labels = {label: 0 for label in sorted(LABELS)}
    for shard_path in shards:
        shard = read(shard_path)
        if {key: shard[key] for key in envelope} != envelope:
            raise ValueError(f"metadata changed in {shard_path}")
        for item in shard["suggestions"]:
            key = pair(item)
            if key not in expected:
                raise ValueError(f"unexpected suggestion in {shard_path}: {key}")
            if key in merged:
                raise ValueError(f"duplicate curated suggestion: {key}")
            if immutable(item) != immutable(expected[key]):
                raise ValueError(f"immutable fields changed for {key}")
            label = item.get("label")
            if label not in LABELS:
                raise ValueError(f"invalid or missing label for {key}: {label!r}")
            rationale = str(item.get("rationale", "")).strip()
            if len(rationale) < 20:
                raise ValueError(f"rationale too short for {key}")
            audit = item.get("audit")
            if not isinstance(audit, dict) or set(audit) != AUDIT_FIELDS:
                raise ValueError(f"invalid audit shape for {key}")
            for field in AUDIT_FIELDS - {"target_sha256"}:
                if not isinstance(audit[field], str) or not audit[field].strip():
                    raise ValueError(f"empty audit field {field} for {key}")
            verify_target(repository, item)
            labels[label] += 1
            merged[key] = item

    missing = sorted(set(expected) - set(merged))
    if missing:
        raise ValueError(f"missing {len(missing)} curated suggestions; first: {missing[:3]}")
    result = dict(envelope)
    result["corpus"] = dict(result["corpus"])
    result["corpus"]["reviewed_at"] = reviewed_at
    result["suggestions"] = [merged[pair(item)] for item in template["suggestions"]]
    output.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    output.write_text(json.dumps(result, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
    return labels


def main() -> int:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
    parser.add_argument("--source", type=Path, required=True)
    parser.add_argument("--repository", type=Path, required=True)
    parser.add_argument("--output", type=Path, required=True)
    parser.add_argument("--reviewed-at", required=True)
    parser.add_argument("shards", nargs="+", type=Path)
    args = parser.parse_args()
    labels = merge(args.source, args.shards, args.repository.resolve(), args.output, args.reviewed_at)
    print(json.dumps({"output": str(args.output), "labels": labels}, sort_keys=True))
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())

ok {\n            plan.Rewrites = append(plan.Rewrites, rewrite.rewrite)\n            plan.Updates = append(plan.Updates, rewrite.update)\n            plan.Links.Links = append(plan.Links.Links, rewrite.records...)\n            plan.Messages = append(plan.Messages, rewrite.messages...)\n            continue\n        }\n        previousSource := previousByID[source.record.ID]\n        previousRecords := previousBySource[source.record.ID]\n        if initialized && sourceUnchanged(previousSource, source.record) && recordsReusable(previousRecords) {\n            for _, record := range previousRecords {\n                record.SourcePath = source.record.Path\n                plan.Links.Links = append(plan.Links.Links, record)\n            }\n            continue\n        }\n        if err := reconcileMarkdownSource(&plan, inventory, source, previousRecords, initialized); def incomplete(paths: list[Path]) -> None:\\r\\n            value = read_json(paths[2])\\r\\n            value[\\\"suggestions\\\"] = value[\\\"suggestions\\\"][:-1]\\r\\n            write_json(paths[2], value, force=True)\\r\\n\\r\\n        expect_failure(\\\"duplicate\\\", duplicate)\\r\\n        expect_failure(\\\"missing-label\\\", missing_label)\\r\\n        expect_failure(\\\"wrong-metadata\\\", wrong_metadata)\\r\\n        expect_failure(\\\"incomplete\\\", incomplete)\\r\\n\\r\\n\\r\\ndef parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:\\r\\n    common = merge_parser.add_argument(\\\"--shards\\\", nargs=SHARD_COUNT, type=Path, help=\\\"three shard paths, in any order\\\")\\r\\n    check_parser = commands.add_parser(\\\"check\\\", parents=[common], help=\\\"run split/merge self-checks\\\")\\r\\n    return command_parser\\r\\n\\r\\n\\r\\ndef main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:\\r\\n    args = parser().parse_args(argv)\\r\\n    try:\\r\\n codemap precision curation shards.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\r\\n\\r\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\r\\n\\r\\nimport argparse\\r\\nimport hashlib\\r\\nimport json\\r\\nfrom pathlib import Path\\r\\nfrom typing import Any\\r\\n\\r\\nLABELS = {\\\"valid_missing_link\\\", \\\"plausible_but_unnecessary\\\", \\\"incorrect\\\"}\\r\\nAUDIT_FIELDS = {\\r\\n    \\\"document_section\\\",\\r\\n    \\\"document_ref\\\",\\r\\n    \\\"document_excerpt\\\",\\r\\n    \\\"target_ref\\\",\\r\\n    \\\"target_excerpt\\\",\\r\\n    \\\"target_sha256\\\",\\r\\n    \\\"target_kind\\\",\\r\\n}\\r\\nMUTABLE_FIELDS = {\\\"label\\\", \\\"rationale\\\", \\\"audit\\\"}\\r\\n\\r\\n\\r\\ndef read(path: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:\\r\\n    with path.open(encoding=\\\"utf-8\\\") as stream:\\r\\n        value = json.load(stream)\\r\\n    if not isinstance(value, dict) or not isinstance(value.get(\\\"suggestions\\\"), list):\\r\\n        raise ValueError(f\\\"invalid benchmark JSON: {path}\\\")\\r\\n    return value\\r\\n\\r\\n\\r\\ndef pair(item: dict[str, Any]) -> tuple[str, str]:\\r\\n    return str(item.get(\\\"document\\\", \\\"\\\")), str(item.get(\\\"target\\\", \\\"\\\"))\\r\\n\\r\\n\\r\\ndef immutable(item: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:\\r\\n    return {key: value for key, value in item.items() if key not in MUTABLE_FIELDS}\\r\\n\\r\\n\\r\\ndef file_hash(path: Path) -> str:\\r\\n    digest = hashlib.sha256()\\r\\n    with path.open(\\\"rb\\\") as stream:\\r\\n        for block in iter(lambda: stream.read(1024 * 1024), b\\\"\\\"):\\r\\n            digest.update(block)\\r\\n    return digest.hexdigest()\\r\\n\\r\\n\\r\\ndef directory_hash(root: Path, directory: Path) -> str:\\r\\n    rows: list[str] = []\\r\\n    for child in sorted(path for path in directory.rglob(\\\"*\\\") if path.is_file()):\\r\\n        rows.append(f\\\"{child.relative_to(root).as_posix()}:{file_hash(child)}\\\")\\r\\n    return hashlib.sha256(\\\"\\\\n\\\".join(rows).encode()).hexdigest()\\r\\n\\r\\n\\r\\ndef verify_target(root: Path, item: dict[str, Any]) -> None:\\r\\n    audit = item[\\\"audit\\\"]\\r\\n    target = root.joinpath(*str(item[\\\"target\\\"]).split(\\\"/\\\"))\\r\\n    kind = audit[\\\"target_kind\\\"]\\r\\n    expected = audit[\\\"target_sha256\\\"]\\r\\n    if target.is_file():\\r\\n        if kind != \\\"file\\\":\\r\\n            raise ValueError(f\\\"{pair(item)} target kind {kind!r}, expected file\\\")\\r\\n        actual = file_hash(target)\\r\\n    elif target.is_dir():\\r\\n        if kind != \\\"directory\\\":\\r\\n            raise ValueError(f\\\"{pair(item)} target kind {kind!r}, expected directory\\\")\\r\\n        actual = directory_hash(root, target)\\r\\n    else:\\r\\n        if kind != \\\"missing\\\":\\r\\n            raise ValueError(f\\\"{pair(item)} target does not exist but kind is {kind!r}\\\")\\r\\n        actual = \\\"\\\"\\r\\n    if expected != actual:\\r\\n        raise ValueError(f\\\"{pair(item)} target SHA mismatch: {expected!r} != {actual!r}\\\")\\r\\n\\r\\n\\r\\ndef merge(source: Path, shards: list[Path], repository: Path, output: Path) -> dict[str, int]:\\r\\n    template = read(source)\\r\\n    envelope = {key: template[key] for key in (\\\"schema_version\\\", \\\"corpus\\\", \\\"sampling\\\")}\\r\\n    expected = {pair(item): item for item in template[\\\"suggestions\\\"]}\\r\\n    if len(expected) != len(template[\\\"suggestions\\\"]):\\r\\n        raise ValueError(\\\"template contains duplicate document-target pairs\\\")\\r\\n\\r\\n    merged: dict[tuple[str, str], dict[str, Any]] = {}\\r\\n    labels = {label: 0 for label in sorted(LABELS)}\\r\\n    for shard_path in shards:\\r\\n        shard = read(shard_path)\\r\\n        if {key: shard[key] for key in envelope} != envelope:\\r\\n {key}\\\")\\r\\n            if immutable(item) != immutable(expected[key]):\\r\\n                raise ValueError(f\\\"immutable fields changed for {key}\\\")\\r\\n            label = item.get(\\\"label\\\")\\r\\n            if label not in LABELS:\\r\\n                raise ValueError(f\\\"invalid or missing label for {key}: {label!r}\\\")\\r\\n            rationale = result[\\\"suggestions\\\"] = [merged[pair(item)] for item in template[\\\"suggestions\\\"]]\\r\\n    output.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\\r\\n    output.write_text(json.dumps(result, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False) + \\\"\\\\n\\\", encoding=\\\"utf-8\\\")\\r\\n    return labels\\r\\n\\r\\n\\r\\ndef main() -> int:\\r\\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)\\r\\n SystemExit(main())\\r\\n--- README relevant ---\\r\\n\\r\\nThis benchmark measures the quality of ordinary missing-link suggestions from the current Demon Docs pipeline. It complements, and does not modify, the positive-only recall benchmark in [`research/codemap-review/space-rocks-trusted-links.json`](../codemap-review/space-rocks-trusted-links.json).\\r\\n\\r\\n## Corpus and sampling\\r\\n\\r\\n- Corpus: Space Rocks revision `3387c94d10fdb94008f27b404098f3e0c32d911c` (10 reviewed documents, 51 trusted links).\\r\\n- Source run: all 51 trusted links were hidden with seed `space-rocks-precision-v1`; the run produced 267 unique suggestions, including 24 recovered trusted links and 243 ordinary unmatched suggestions.\\r\\n- Sample: 150 unique document-target pairs in [`space-rocks-precision-benchmark.json`](space-rocks-precision-benchmark.json).\\r\\n- Sampling: every document’s top five was retained where available; the remainder was selected deterministically with SHA-256 least-represented fill across document area, score bucket, primary evidence kind, and rank bucket.\\r\\n- Labels were assigned after inspecting the referenced document section and target file or directory. Each row records document and target references, excerpts, target kind, a target fingerprint, evidence, and a concise rationale.\\r\\n\\r\\nThe three labels are mutually exclusive: `valid_missing_link` is correct for strict precision, `plausible_but_unnecessary` is accepted only for acceptance precision, and `incorrect` is junk.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Reproduction\\r\\n\\r\\nGenerate the source suggestion report without changing Space Rocks:\\r\\n\\r\\n```text\\r\\ngo run ./cmd/ddocs codemap benchmark --repo D:\\\\!bin\\\\space-rocks --trusted-links research\\\\codemap-review\\\\space-rocks-trusted-links.json --holdout-count 51 --seed space-rocks-precision-v1 --format json --output tmp\\\\codemap-precision\\\\full-hidden.json\\r\\n```\\r\\n\\r\\nEvaluate the labeled sample:\\r\\n\\r\\n```text\\r\\ngo run ./cmd/ddocs codemap precision --benchmark research\\\\codemap-precision\\\\space-rocks-precision-benchmark.json --suggestions tmp\\\\codemap-precision\\\\full-hidden.json\\r\\n```\\r\\n\\r\\nThe evaluator verifies that every sampled pair, score/evidence payload, and rank still exists in the source report before calculating metrics.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Results\\r\\n\\r\\nEvaluation date: 2026-07-19.\\r\\n\\r\\n| metric | result |\\r\\n| --- | four document areas: data (26), devtools (41), protocol (33), and services (50). Subsystem counts are recorded in the evaluator’s JSON output and the row-level artifact.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Limitations\\r\\n\\r\\nThis is a curated sample, not an unbiased estimate over every repository path. It is drawn from a full-hidden run of the 51-link trusted corpus, so it evaluates the current evidence pipeline under that benchmark setup and does not measure recall or unseen-document behavior. Directory suggestions are retained as ordinary candidates but are judged conservatively. Labels are auditable and source-pinned, but this artifact has one review pass rather than independent inter-rater agreement. The existing recall benchmark remains the appropriate measure for recovery of known \\r\\n  15: The active `tools/data_sync` pipeline currently owns generation and drift checks for:\\r\\n  16: \\r\\n  17: ```text\\r\\n  18: constants\\r\\n  19: packets\\r\\n  20: drop_tables\\r\\n Pipeline usage\\r\\n 271: \\r\\n 272: Exactly one data-sync operation must be selected:\\r\\n 273: \\r\\n 274: ```text\\r\\n 275: -push\\r\\n 276: -pull\\r\\n 277: -diff\\r\\n 278: -check\\r\\n 279: -validate\\r\\n 280: ```\\r\\n 281: \\r\\n 282: Supported active generation domains are:\\r\\n 283: \\r\\n 284: ```text\\r\\n 285: -constants\\r\\n 286: -packets\\r\\n 287: -drop-tables\\r\\n 288: -realtime-wire\\r\\n 289: -observability\\r\\n 290: ```\\r\\n 291: \\r\\n 292: `-player_data` is currently validation-only.\\r\\n 293: \\r\\n 294: Supported language flags are:\\r\\n 295: \\r\\n 296: ```text\\r\\n 297: -go\\r\\n 298: -gds\\r\\n 299: -ts\\r\\n 300: -ruby\\r\\n 301: ```\\r\\n 302: \\r\\n 303: For `-realtime-wire`, `-go`, `-gds`, `-json`, and `-docs` select the generated outputs. For `-observability`, `-go`, `-gds`, `-ruby`, `-json`, and `-docs` select the generated outputs. `-json` and `-docs` apply to both realtime wire and observability; they are output selectors, not programming languages or general language targets for every domain.\\r\\n 304: \\r\\n 305: `-push` writes generated output.\\r\\n 306: \\r\\n 307: ```text\\r\\n 308: data-sync -push -constants -go -gds\\r\\n 309: data-sync -push -packets -go -gds\\r\\n 310: data-sync -push -drop-tables -go\\r\\n 311: data-sync -push -realtime-wire -go -gds -json -docs\\r\\n 312: data-sync -push -observability -go -gds -ruby -json -docs\\r\\n 313: ```\\r\\n 314: \\r\\n 315: `-diff` renders generated output and prints a unified diff without writing.\\r\\n 316: \\r\\n 317: ```text\\r\\n 318: data-sync -diff -constants -go -gds\\r\\n 319: data-sync -diff -packets -go -gds\\r\\n 320: data-sync -diff -drop-tables -go\\r\\n 321: data-sync -diff -realtime-wire -go -gds -json -docs\\r\\n 322: data-sync -diff -observability -go -gds -ruby -json -docs\\r\\n 323: ```\\r\\n 324: \\r\\n 325: `-check` renders generated output, writes nothing, exits `0` when outputs are current, and exits `1` when generated files differ.\\r\\n 326: \\r\\n 327: ```text\\r\\n 328: data-sync -check -constants -go -gds\\r\\n 329: data-sync -check -packets -go -gds\\r\\n 330: data-sync -check -drop-tables -go\\r\\n 331: data-sync -check -realtime-wire -go -gds -json -docs\\r\\n 332: data-sync -check -observability -go -gds -ruby -json -docs\\r\\n 333: ```\\r\\n 334: \\r\\n 335: `-validate` checks configuration, source TOML structure, supported values, configured files, managed blocks, packet schemas, drop-table schemas, and player-data logical schema where requested.\\r\\n 336: \\r\\n 337: ```text\\r\\n 338: data-sync -validate\\r\\n 339: data-sync -validate -constants\\r\\n 340: data-sync -validate -packets\\r\\n 341: data-sync -validate -realtime-wire\\r\\n 342: data-sync -validate -drop-tables\\r\\n 343: data-sync -validate -player_data\\r\\n 344: data-sync -validate -observability\\r\\n 345: ```\\r\\n 346: \\r\\n 347: `-pull` is restricted to constants and one language at a time.\\r\\n 348: \\r\\n 349: ```text\\r\\n 350: data-sync -pull -constants -go\\r\\n 351: data-sync -pull -constants -gds\\r\\n 352: ```\\r\\n 353: \\r\\n 354: Packet pull is intentionally unsupported. Packet shape changes must be made in `shared/packets/*.toml` and pushed outward.\\r\\n 355: \\r\\n 356: Drop-table pull is not the active workflow. Drop-table changes should be made in `shared/drop_tables/*.toml`.\\r\\n 357: \\r\\n 358: Player-data schema push/pull/diff/check is not implemented. `-player_data` is validation-only.\\r\\n 359: \\r\\n 360: ## Validation commands\\r\\n 361: \\r\\n 362: Project-wide data-sync validation:\\r\\n 363: \\r\\n 364: ```text\\r\\n 365: data-sync -validate\\r\\n 366: ```\\r\\n 367: \\r\\n 368: Constants drift check:\\r\\n 369: \\r\\n 370: ```text\\r\\n 371: data-sync -check -constants -go -gds\\r\\n 372: ```\\r\\n 373: \\r\\n 374: Packet drift check:\\r\\n 375: \\r\\n 376: ```text\\r\\n 377: data-sync -check -packets -go -gds\\r\\n 378: ```\\r\\n 379: \\r\\n 380: Drop-table drift check:\\r\\n 381: \\r\\n 382: ```text\\r\\n 383: data-sync -check -drop-tables -go\\r\\n 384: ```\\r\\n 385: \\r\\n 386: Realtime-wire validation and drift check:\\r\\n 387: \\r\\n 388: ```text\\r\\n 389: data-sync -validate -realtime-wire\\r\\n 390: data-sync -check -realtime-wire -go -gds -json -docs\\r\\n 391: data-sync -check -observability -go -gds -ruby -json -docs\\r\\n 392: ```\\r\\n 393: \\r\\n 394: Player-data logical schema validation:\\r\\n 395: \\r\\n 396: ```text\\r\\n 397: data-sync -validate -player_data\\r\\n 398: data-sync -validate -observability\\r\\n 399: ```\\r\\n 400: \\r\\n 401: Tool test suite:\\r\\n 402: \\r\\n 403: ```text\\r\\n 404: cd tools/data_sync && python -m pytest\\r\\n 405: ```\\r\\n 406: \\r\\n 407: Game-server validation after generated server constants, packets, drop tables, asteroid data, or collision data change:\\r\\n 408: \\r\\n 409: ```text\\r\\n 410: cd services/game-server && go test -buildvcs=false ./...\\r\\n 411: ```\\r\\n 412: \\r\\n 413: Player-data validation after player-data packets or logical player-data schema change:\\r\\n 414: \\r\\n 415: ```text\\r\\n 416: cd services/player-data && go test ./...\\r\\n 417: ```\\r\\n 418: \\r\\n 419: HTTP contract validation after OpenAPI changes:\\r\\n 420: \\r\\n 421: ```text\\r\\n 422: cd services/api-server && bundle exec rails test test/contracts/openapi_contract_test.rb\\r\\n 423: ```\\r\\n 424: \\r\\n 425: Collision-shape export validation uses the Godot export tool, not data-sync:\\r\\n 426: \\r\\n 427: ```text\\r\\n 428: godot --headless --path client -s res://tools/export_collision_shapes.gd\\r\\n 429: ```\\r\\n 430: \\r\\n 431: ## Failure modes\\r\\n 432: \\r\\n 433: Duplicate constants sections across multiple source TOML files fail validation or generation. Each constants section must have exactly one source owner.\\r\\n 434: \\r\\n 435: Missing constants sections fail when a generated destination block references a section that does not exist in the constants source set.\\r\\n 436: \\r\\n 437: Malformed or ambiguous managed blocks fail constants sync. Missing start markers, missing end markers, duplicate blocks, or mismatched marker comment styles are hard failures.\\r\\n 438: \\r\\n 439: Unsupported constants values fail validation. Constants support booleans, integers, floats, strings, recursively formatted GDScript arrays and string-keyed dictionaries, including structured ICE-server-shaped values with nested URL arrays. A two-number numeric array remains a `Vector2` value for GDScript output. Unsupported nested values and non-string dictionary keys fail GDScript generation.\\r\\n 440: \\r\\n 441: Packet schema errors fail validation or generation. Common failures include duplicate packet type IDs, duplicate packet values, invalid struct names, invalid JSON field names, unsupported field types, unknown struct references, unknown packet output IDs, or references to unknown packet builder fields.\\r\\n 442: \\r\\n 443: Packet target errors fail generation. A configured packet target must be enabled, must have a matching output in `shared/packets/outputs.toml`, and must use a supported language renderer.\\r\\n 444: \\r\\n 445: Realtime-wire validation fails for invalid logical packet, struct, or field references; alias collisions; invalid tuple layouts; missing quantization, ID, or event declarations; and inconsistent packet, lane, record, or compatibility metadata. Generated drift is reported by the realtime-wire check command.\\r\\n 446: \\r\\n 447: Physical realtime-wire declarations that reference unknown logical packet, struct, or field names fail validation.\\r\\n 448: \\r\\n 449: Duplicate or non-invertible aliases fail validation.\\r\\n 450: \\r\\n 451: Invalid record, fixed-tuple, sparse-tuple, or sparse-positional layouts fail validation.\\r\\n 452: \\r\\n 453: Missing or invalid quantization, ID codec or selector, event, or decode-alternative references fail validation.\\r\\n 454: \\r\\n 455: Generated Go, GDScript, JSON, or documentation drift is reported by the realtime-wire check command.\\r\\n 456: \\r\\n 457: Observability validation fails for malformed contract TOML, duplicate services/events/fields, invalid service allowlists or trace requirements, unsupported field types or limits, ambiguous redaction rules, unknown retention/diagnostic-bundle references, missing configured targets, or non-deterministic generated output. Its generated Go, GDScript, Ruby, JSON, and Markdown drift is reported by the observability check command. Runtime rejection/write failures are bounded emitter outcomes and are distinct from data-sync failures.\\r\\n 458: \\r\\n 459: Packet pull fails by design. Packet schemas are edited in TOML, not reconstructed from generated Go or GDScript.\\r\\n 460: \\r\\n 461: Drop-table generation is Go-only. `-drop-tables` requires `-go`.\\r\\n 462: \\r\\n 463: Player-data schema currently validates only a limited logical schema shape. Supported player-data field types are:\\r\\n 464: \\r\\n 465: ```text\\r\\n 466: string\\r\\n 467: integer\\r\\n 468: boolean\\r\\n 469: ```\\r\\n 470: \\r\\n 471: Each player-data field must define `required`, `optional`, or `default`.\\r\\n 472: \\r\\n 473: TypeScript packet output is disabled in the default configuration. A command that tries to generate disabled packet targets should fail instead of silently producing partial output.\\r\\n 474: \\r\\n 475: Editing generated files directly creates drift. The correct fix is to update the owning source and rerun the appropriate data-sync command.\\r\\n 476: \\r\\n 477: Treating OpenAPI, Rails migrations, collision exports, or Godot scene data as data-sync-owned material creates ownership confusion. Those are adjacent source-of-truth paths with separate validation workflows.\\r\\n 478: \\r\\n 479: ## Code or source map\\r\\n 480: \\r\\n 481: CLI entry and orchestration:\\r\\n 482: \\r\\n 483: ```text\\r\\n 484: tools/data_sync/main.py\\r\\n 485: tools/data_sync/data_sync/cli.py\\r\\n 486: tools/data_sync/data_sync/config.py\\r\\n 487: tools/data_sync/data_sync/validate.py\\r\\n 488: means source of truth. It does not imply every source of truth is handled by `tools/data_sync`.\\r\\n 614: \\r\\n 615: The data-sync pipeline is intentionally narrower than the project-wide source-of-truth map. Constants, packets, drop tables, realtime wire, and observability are active generated domains. Player-data logical schema is validate-only. HTTP contracts, collision exports, asteroid variants, Rails migrations, and Godot scenes use separate ownership and verification paths.\\r\\n===== docs/data/observability-contract.md =====\\r\\n   1: # Observability Contract\\r\\n   2: \\r\\n   3: Parent index: [Data](./!INDEX.md)\\r\\n   4: \\r\\n   5: ## Purpose and ownership\\r\\n   6: \\r\\n   7: Observability is an active `tools/data_sync` source-of-truth domain. The editable contract is the TOML source set below. `data-sync` validates the cross-file model and generates all language/runtime metadata and the generated contract reference. Runtime emitters consume generated metadata; generated outputs are never edited manually.\\r\\n   8: \\r\\n   9: ## Source files\\r\\n  10: \\r\\n  11: ```text\\r\\n  12: shared/contracts/observability/schema.toml\\r\\n  13: shared/contracts/observability/services.toml\\r\\n  14: shared/contracts/observability/events.toml\\r\\n  15: shared/contracts/observability/fields.toml\\r\\n  16: shared/contracts/observability/redaction.toml\\r\\n  17: shared/contracts/observability/retention_tiers.toml\\r\\n  18: shared/contracts/observability/diagnostic_bundle.toml\\r\\n  19: ```\\r\\n  20: \\r\\n  21: Together these files define the envelope, service registry, event eligibility and defaults, context/free-form fields, redaction policy, retention tiers, and diagnostic-bundle contract. No service may add a local event definition or policy that changes these generated rules.\\r\\n  22: \\r\\n  23: ## Generated outputs\\r\\n  24: \\r\\n  25: ```text\\r\\n  26: shared/go/observabilityevent/contract_generated.go\\r\\n  27: client/scripts/generated/observability/contract_generated.gd\\r\\n  28: services/api-server/app/lib/observability/contract_generated.rb\\r\\n  29: shared/contracts/observability/generated/contract.json\\r\\n  30: docs/observability/generated/contract-reference.md\\r\\n  31: ```\\r\\n  32: \\r\\n  33: The generated Markdown reference is a read-only rendered reference. It changes only through the observability data-sync generator.\\r\\n  34: \\r\\n  35: ## Commands\\r\\n  36: \\r\\n  37: From the repository root:\\r\\n  38: \\r\\n  39: ```bash\\r\\n  40: data-sync -validate -observability\\r\\n  41: data-sync -diff -observability -go -gds -ruby -json -docs\\r\\n  42: data-sync -push -observability -go -gds -ruby -json -docs\\r\\n  43: data-sync -check -observability -go -gds -ruby -json -docs\\r\\n  44: ```\\r\\n  45: \\r\\n  46: `-validate` reads and cross-validates the source contract without writing. `-diff` renders every selected output and reports drift. `-push` writes selected generated outputs. `-check` renders without writing and fails when any selected output differs.\\r\\n  47: \\r\\n  48: ## Validation rules\\r\\n  49: \\r\\n  50: Validation covers:\\r\\n  51: \\r\\n  52: - one valid schema/envelope definition and supported schema version;\\r\\n  53: - unique service keys and emitted names;\\r\\n  54: - unique event names, categories, levels, service allowlists, trace requirements, and bridge-only declarations;\\r\\n  55: - context field names, types, UUID requirements, and limits;\\r\\n  56: - free-form key/value/count/size limits;\\r\\n  57: - redaction exact/fragment rules and rejection of ambiguous actions;\\r\\n  58: - retention tier references and file policy values;\\r\\n  59: - diagnostic-bundle field/event references;\\r\\n  60: - deterministic output ordering and complete configured target files.\\r\\n  61: \\r\\n  62: Generation fails rather than silently dropping an unsupported language or output. The Ruby selector is observability-specific; JSON and docs selectors apply to observability as well as realtime wire.\\r\\n  63: \\r\\n  64: ## Generation ownership and consumers\\r\\n  65: \\r\\n  66: `tools/data_sync/config.toml` owns the `[sot.observability]` source list and the `observability.go`, `observability.gds`, `observability.ruby`, `observability.json`, and `observability.docs` targets. The tool implementation and generators are the only owners of generated output content.\\r\\n  67: \\r\\n  68: Consumers are:\\r\\n  69: \\r\\n  70: - Go services through `shared/go/observabilityevent` and service `logging.Emit` boundaries;\\r\\n  71: - the Godot client through `observability_emitter.gd` and generated GDScript constants;\\r\\n  72: - API Rails through `Observability::Emitter`, `WorkerRuntime`, and `PumaHooks`;\\r\\n  73: - diagnostic-aggregator through generated Go observability metadata;\\r\\n  74: - handwritten service/data docs through this document and the generated contract reference.\\r\\n  75: \\r\\n  76: ## Failure modes\\r\\n  77: \\r\\n  78: - malformed TOML or a missing source file fails validation;\\r\\n  79: - duplicate service/event/field/retention identifiers fail validation;\\r\\n  80: - an event referencing an unknown service, field, or retention tier fails validation;\\r\\n  81: - invalid trace/UUID, field type, redaction, or limit declarations fail validation;\\r\\n  82: - unsupported selector combinations fail CLI parsing;\\r\\n  83: - generated output drift causes `-check` to exit nonzero;\\r\\n  84: - a runtime emitter rejects an event, unsafe value, or oversized record according to generated rejection codes;\\r\\n  85: - a writer failure degrades the service-owned runtime but must not cause the workflow to raise or serialize the rejected/private payload.\\r\\n  86: \\r\\n  87: The contract pipeline and runtime writer failures are separate: data-sync failure blocks a generated-contract change; a runtime write failure is a bounded service degradation.\\r\\n  88: \\r\\n  89: ## Rule against manual edits\\r\\n  90: \\r\\n  91: Do not edit any generated output directly. Change the owning TOML source, run validation, review the diff, push the selected outputs, and run the check command. A manual edit to a generated Go, GDScript, Ruby, JSON, or Markdown file is drift and must be overwritten by the next push.\\r\\n  92: \\r\\n  93: ## Code and test map\\r\\n  94: \\r\\n  95: ```text\\r\\n  96: tools/data_sync/config.toml\\r\\n  97: tools/data_sync/main.py\\r\\n  98: tools/data_sync/data_sync/cli.py\\r\\n  99: tools/data_sync/data_sync/config.py\\r\\n 100: tools/data_sync/data_sync/observability_toml.py\\r\\n 101: tools/data_sync/data_sync/observability_validate.py\\r\\n 102: tools/data_sync/data_sync/observability_sync.py\\r\\n 103: tools/data_sync/data_sync/model/observability.py\\r\\n 104: tools/data_sync/data_sync/generators/observability_go.py\\r\\n 105: tools/data_sync/data_sync/generators/observability_gds.py\\r\\n 106: tools/data_sync/data_sync/generators/observability_ruby.py\\r\\n 107: tools/data_sync/data_sync/generators/observability_json.py\\r\\n 108: tools/data_sync/data_sync/generators/observability_docs.py\\r\\n 109: tools/data_sync/tests/test_observability_toml.py\\r\\n 110: tools/data_sync/tests/test_observability_validate.py\\r\\n 111: tools/data_sync/tests/test_observability_sync.py\\r\\n 112: tools/data_sync/tests/test_observability_generators.py\\r\\n 113: tools/data_sync/tests/test_observability_emission_guards.py\\r\\n 114: ```\\r\\n 115: \\r\\n 116: ## Related docs\\r\\n 117: \\r\\n 118: - [Data sync and SSoT pipeline](data-sync-and-ssot-pipeline.md)\\r\\n 119: - [Source-of-truth map](source-of-truth-map.md)\\r\\n 120: - [Canonical event emission](../observability/canonical-event-emission.md)\\r\\n 121: - [Generated contract reference](../observability/generated/contract-reference.md)\\r\\n\",\"exit_code\":0,\"status\":\"completed\"}}\n{\"type\":\"item.completed\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_5\",\"type\":\"agent_message\",\"text\":\"The benchmark contains exactly 37 entries: 20 for the data-sync/SSoT document and 17 for the observability contract.
