Developer Onboarding\\r\\n5:## Purpose\\r\\n11:This document is not the source of truth for detailed system behavior. Detailed facts belong in the owning service, protocol, data, devtools, domain, systems-design, planning, or limits docs.\\r\\n13:## Overview\\r\\n21:shared/                 Shared source-of-truth data\\r\\n22:tools/data_sync/        Constants, packet, and generated-output sync tooling\\r\\n32:## Basic Setup\\r\\n55:Git LFS           binary/source asset files\\r\\n76:## Verification quickstart\\r\\n102:## Repository Shape\\r\\n104:`client/` contains the active Godot client project: scenes, scripts, assets, tests, generated client packet helpers, and generated constants.\\r\\n110:`shared/` contains shared source files used by generators and multiple runtimes, including constants, packet schemas, collision data, and related source-of-truth material.\\r\\n112:`tools/data_sync/` contains the data-sync tooling used to validate, diff, and regenerate shared outputs.\\r\\n120:`SourceAssets/` contains local source art material and is ignored by Git.\\r\\n122:## Development Tools\\r\\n124:### data-sync\\r\\n126:`data-sync` is the repo generation and synchronization tool for shared data.\\r\\n128:It is used for packet schemas, constants, generated Go outputs, generated GDScript outputs, and related shared-source validation.\\r\\n136:Use data-sync when changing shared packet or constants sources. Do not hand-edit generated outputs as the source of truth.\\r\\n138:Observability is also an active data-sync domain. Edit only `shared/contracts/observability/*.toml`, then run:\\r\\n141:data-sync -validate -observability\\r\\n142:data-sync -diff -observability -go -gds -ruby -json -docs\\r\\n143:data-sync -push -observability -go -gds -ruby -json -docs\\r\\n144:data-sync -check -observability -go -gds -ruby -json -docs\\r\\n147:See [Observability Contract](data/observability-contract.md) for the source/output owner map.\\r\\n152:data-sync -check -packets -go -gds\\r\\n153:data-sync -check -constants -go -gds\\r\\n159:data-sync -diff -packets -go -gds\\r\\n160:data-sync -diff -constants -go -gds\\r\\n166:data-sync -push -packets -go -gds\\r\\n167:data-sync -push -constants -go -gds\\r\\n170:Use `data-sync` for generated outputs. Do not use it as a replacement for understanding ownership. The owning data docs explain the source files and generated outputs.\\r\\n174:`doc-ledger` maintains the generated sections in documentation indexes.\\r\\n191:Do not manually fight generated index blocks. Manual index text belongs outside the doc-ledger marker blocks. If an index is wrong, fix the underlying file/folder placement, doc-ledger configuration, or generated description source rather than hand-editing only the generated block.\\r\\n212:Use `bruno-api/` for real local API-server routes and `bruno-diagnostic-aggregator/` for hosted diagnostic aggregator checks. Keep their collections and environments separate. Bruno does not replace Rails tests, does not own HTTP contracts, does not bypass application behavior, and must not contain committed secrets.\\r\\n249:Git LFS is required for binary and source asset files.\\r\\n338:set -a && source ../../.env && set +a\\r\\n368:Use Python tooling for repo validation and generation support. Do not use ad hoc Python scripts to mutate source-of-truth data when an existing repo tool owns the workflow.\\r\\n378:{ (cd services/game-server && set -a && source ../../.env && set +a && air); }\\r\\n383:## MCP Servers And Godot Bridge\\r\\n415:## Running The Project\\r\\n422:- [Player-data observability](services/player-data/observability-and-logging.md)\\r\\n423:- [API-server observability](services/api-server/observability-and-logging.md)\\r\\n432:{ (cd services/game-server && set -a && source ../../.env && set +a && go run ./cmd/game-server); }\\r\\n452:{ (cd services/api-server && set -a && source ../../.env && set +a && bundle exec rails server); }\\r\\n461:set -a && source ../../.env && set +a\\r\\n483:## Verifying Changes\\r\\n504:set -a && source ../../.env && set +a\\r\\n511:data-sync -check -packets -go -gds\\r\\n512:data-sync -check -constants -go -gds\\r\\n515:For generated-output diffs before pushing:\\r\\n518:data-sync -diff -packets -go -gds\\r\\n519:data-sync -diff -constants -go -gds\\r\\n541:## Documentation Map\\r\\n546:* [Data](data/!INDEX.md) - Source-of-truth files, generated outputs, schemas, persistence contracts, and data-sync pipeline documentation.\\r\\n551:* [Protocol](protocol/!INDEX.md) - HTTP, WebSocket, packet, and message-flow contracts between systems.\\r\\n557:## Source-Of-Truth Rules\\r\\n559:Generated files are not the source of truth.\\r\\n581:Use data-sync to check, diff, and regenerate generated outputs.\\r\\n583:Protocol docs own message and API contracts.\\r\\n589:Data docs own schemas, persistence contracts, source files, generated outputs, and pipeline behavior.\\r\\n597:## Development Cautions\\r\\n599:Do not hand-edit generated packet or constants outputs as source-of-truth changes.\\r\\n605:Do not treat legacy docs as current authority. Legacy docs are migration source material only.\\r\\n617:## Handoff Notes\\r\\n626:* Generated packet and constants files should be regenerated through data-sync, not hand-edited as the source.\\r\\n631:## Related docs\\r\\n640:* [Source-of-truth map](data/source-of-truth-map.md)\\r\\n641:* [Data sync and source-of-truth pipeline](data/data-sync-and-ssot-pipeline.md)\\r\\n651:## def blob(path):
    return subprocess.check_output(['git','-C',repo,'cat-file','blob',f'{rev}:{path}'])

def sha(path):
    return hashlib.sha256(blob(path)).hexdigest()

def dsha(path):

    return hashlib.sha256('\\n'.join(rows).encode()).hexdigest()

repo = r'D:\\"'!bin'"\\space-rocks'

def blob(path):
    return subprocess.check_output(['git','-C',repo,'cat-file','blob',f'{rev}:{path}'])

def sha(path):
    return hashlib.sha256(blob(path)).hexdigest()

def dsha(path):
    files = subprocess.check_output(['git','-C',repo,'ls-tree','-r','--name-only',rev,'--',path.rstrip('/')], text=True).splitlines()
    rows = [f'{f}:{sha(f)}' for f in files]
    return hashlib.sha256('\\n'.join(rows).encode()).hexdigest()
